content.text:
{
  "dimension": "perceptual-accuracy",
  "score": 2,
  "rationale": "The coach demonstrates some perceptual awareness but mostly stays in content-level inquiry. The strongest read is in Turn 2, where the coach reflects back the exact stack of signals ('Six months, a bottle of wine, and I don't really care') — naming the flatness/dissociation in the client's opening. Turn 4 ('is it there right now, as you're saying this?') is a good present-moment somatic check-in on the static. Turn 8 ('You stopped right at the door') names a pattern. However, the coach misses several visible reads: the linguistic caveat 'I'm not here because I think I have a drinking problem, just so we're clear' (a defendedness signal that recurs in Turn 11 as 'I'm not an alcoholic'), the brace/pull-back in Turn 11 ('pulls back a little, more clipped'), the shift to challenging in Turn 13 ('almost challenging'), and the rush signature in the client's logistics-listing in Turn 5. The coach never names these protective moves as they happen. Reads, when present, tend to be content-pattern reflections rather than somatic/stance reads, and key defensive signals go unmarked.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 8, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Coach mirrors the three-part signal stack — duration, quantity, flatness — which functions as a read of the dissociated stance, though not named explicitly."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Present-moment somatic check on the static. Good timing, locates the sensation in the now."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Question is content-level; no read named, though it follows from the prior somatic thread."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names a pattern — 'stopped right at the door' — reading the avoidance structure, not just the content."},
    {"turn": 9, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Client braces and pulls back ('Maybe. Or maybe...') and introduces 'stuff I don't want to look at' — the coach does not name the brace or the caveat."},
    {"turn": 11, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Client pulls back, gets clipped, and reasserts 'I'm not an alcoholic.' This is a clear defendedness signal that the coach does not name in the next turn."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Coach normalizes the relief (good intervention) but does not read the protective clip that just happened."},
    {"turn": 13, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Client becomes 'almost challenging' and bait surfaces. Coach's next turn (14) does not name the challenge or shift in stance."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Warm reframe of love/resentment, but no read on the client's state in the moment."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": [],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["the-rush-signature"],
  "notes": "The coach's reads are mostly content-pattern mirrors rather than somatic/stance/linguistic reads. Defendedness signals ('just so we're clear,' 'I'm not an alcoholic,' the clip-and-challenge) are visible repeatedly and never named, which is the central miss for this dimension."
}
